name: Shahi Korma (Mutton Curry)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean, wash, and dry the goat mutton.
2. Blend coriander seeds, 1 onion, almonds, and garlic together.
3. Marinate the mutton with the blended mixture for at least 2 hours.
4. Heat oil in a pan, fry the remaining thinly sliced onions until golden brown. Set aside.
5. Add the marinated mutton to the pan and cook until the liquid is almost evaporated.
6. Stir in 3/4 cup hot water and simmer on low heat until the meat is nearly cooked through (about 75% done). Alternatively, pressure cook at high pressure for 20 minutes, then reduce pressure and add salt and pepper.
7. Remove from heat and let the sauce reduce slightly.
8. Add beaten yogurt to the pan and stir until oil separates.
9. Incorporate the fried ground onion mixture.
10. Stir in the whipped cream and saffron strands (soaked or ground).
11. Combine everything with the cooked mutton.
12. Finish by stirring in garam masala. Bake at 250°F for an additional half hour, covered loosely.
13. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves before serving.
